explanation.rule: The prefix re- is added to verbs to mean 'again' or 'back', indicating that an action is done a second time or in reverse.

groups.note: Added to verbs to mean 'again' or 'back'. Simply place re- before the base verb with no spelling changes.

examples.definition: to put something back or substitute it with something new
to build something again after it has been damaged
to write something again in a different or better way
to organize something again in a new or better way

wordForms.example: The teacher asked her to redo the exercise more carefully.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to perform an action or task

wordForms.definition: to put something back or substitute with something new
wordForms.example: We need to replace the broken window before winter.

wordForms.definition: to build something again after it has been destroyed or damaged
wordForms.example: The community worked together to rebuild their homes after the flood.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to construct something by putting parts together

wordForms.definition: to come or go back to a place or situation
wordForms.example: She plans to return to university to finish her degree.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to move in a circular direction; to change direction

wordForms.definition: to use something again instead of throwing it away
wordForms.example: We try to reuse plastic bags whenever possible.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to employ something for a purpose

wordForms.definition: to write something again in a different or improved way
wordForms.example: The editor asked the journalist to rewrite the introduction.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to form letters and words on a surface

wordForms.definition: to play a recording or game again
wordForms.example: Let's replay that video clip — I missed the beginning.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to take part in a game or to operate a device for entertainment

wordForms.definition: to start something again after it has stopped
wordForms.example: You may need to restart the computer after the update.

wordForms.definition: to think about a decision or opinion again
wordForms.example: I hope the committee will reconsider their decision.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to think carefully about something before deciding

wordForms.definition: to organize something again in a new or more effective way
wordForms.example: The manager decided to reorganize the whole department.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to arrange things in a structured way

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The students were told to ___ the essay because it was too short.
After the power cut, I had to ___ the computer and open my files again.
The old batteries need to be ___ with new ones.
The referee decided to ___ the match after it was interrupted by rain.
I urge you to ___ your decision before it is too late.
We should ___ bottles and bags rather than throw them away.

transformExercises.sentence: The teacher asked him to ___ the calculation because it contained errors. (DO)
The whole town had to be ___ after the earthquake. (BUILD)
The director plans to ___ the office to make it more efficient. (ORGANIZE)
She decided to ___ to her hometown after living abroad for ten years. (TURN)
Please ___ the device if it stops responding. (START)
